title: Fix label layout: prevent text and barcode overlap, ensure full barcode visibility

requirements.summary: Fix label layout to prevent text and barcode from overlapping on printed labels
acceptanceCriteria: Text elements (prefix, serial number) do not overlap with barcode elements
All text remains fully readable without obstruction
Layout adjustments maintain proper spacing between text and barcode

file_operations.description: Adjust Y-position calculations for text elements (prefix and serial numbers) to ensure adequate vertical spacing above and below the barcode. Modify the generateCPCLForLabel function to position text elements with sufficient margins from barcode boundaries, preventing overlap. Use the LayoutSettings from labelSettings to compute non-overlapping Y-coordinates for titleLayout, serialText1Layout, serialText2Layout, barcode1Layout, and barcode2Layout.

Update the preview rendering logic to accurately reflect the adjusted text and barcode positioning. Ensure that the visual preview matches the actual CPCL output by applying the same Y-position calculations and spacing adjustments used in cpclGenerator.ts. This helps users verify that text and barcodes are properly separated before printing.

requirements.summary: Ensure the complete barcode is visible and scannable on the 58mm × 43mm label
acceptanceCriteria: Entire barcode prints within the label boundaries
No barcode clipping occurs at edges
Barcode remains scannable with standard barcode readers
Barcode width and positioning fit within the 58mm label width

file_operations.description: Enhance the clampBarcodeXPosition function to better handle barcode width calculations and prevent clipping at label edges. Improve the estimateCODE128WidthInDots function to provide more accurate width estimates for different barcode types and data lengths. Add validation to ensure computed barcode X positions and widths fit within the 58mm (463 dots at 203 DPI) label width constraint.

Apply the enhanced clampBarcodeXPosition function when generating CPCL barcode commands. Ensure that barcode X and Y coordinates, along with computed widths, are validated against label boundaries (58mm × 43mm). If a barcode would extend beyond the label edge, adjust its position or scale to fit within bounds while maintaining scannability.

Add diagnostic checks to validate that generated barcodes fit within the 58mm × 43mm label dimensions. Include warnings in diagnostic logs when barcode width or positioning approaches or exceeds label boundaries. This helps identify potential clipping issues before printing.

Update the preview to visually indicate when a barcode would clip at the label edges. Ensure the preview accurately shows the full barcode extent and highlights any overflow beyond the 58mm × 43mm boundaries to alert users before printing.

requirements.summary: Adjust CPCL generation logic to properly position text and barcode elements with sufficient spacing
acceptanceCriteria: CPCL commands position text above or below barcode with adequate margins
Barcode Y-position adjusted to prevent overlap with text elements
Generated CPCL produces cleanly separated text and barcode sections

file_operations.description: Refactor the CPCL command generation to implement a hierarchical layout strategy that positions text and barcode elements with explicit vertical spacing. Use the LayoutSettings (titleLayout, serialText1Layout, serialText2Layout, barcode1Layout, barcode2Layout) and their verticalSpacing properties to calculate non-overlapping Y-coordinates. Ensure that text elements (TEXT commands) are positioned with adequate margins above or below barcode elements (BARCODE commands), preventing any overlap.

Review and adjust the default LayoutSettings values (Y-positions, verticalSpacing) to ensure they provide adequate separation between text and barcode elements for the 58mm × 43mm label. If necessary, update the default configuration to reflect better spacing values that prevent overlap out-of-the-box.

Add validation rules that check for sufficient vertical spacing between text and barcode elements. Ensure that the validation logic flags configurations where text Y-positions overlap with barcode Y-positions plus barcode height, preventing invalid settings from being saved.

Update the label settings UI to provide clearer guidance on Y-position and spacing adjustments. Add helpful tooltips or inline hints that explain how to set Y-coordinates and verticalSpacing to avoid text and barcode overlap. Ensure the preview updates in real-time as users adjust layout settings.
